app.name: Silenium
app.tagline: Isolated local AI: agents, memory, dual mouse

language.title: Choose language
language.subtitle: You can change it anytime in settings

subtitle.chats: Talk to local models and agent teams
subtitle.agents: Build multi-model teams with roles and tools
subtitle.models: Load GGUF from disk or Hugging Face
subtitle.devices: Camera, microphone, screen, and agent browser

welcome.title: Welcome to Silenium
welcome.desc: A next-gen platform for running neural networks locally with full control over data, resources, and AI behavior.

local.title: Local neural networks
local.desc: Run models from Hugging Face or disk. Supports GGUF, ONNX, Safetensors, PT and more.
local.feat1: Hugging Face Hub and local paths

isolation.title: Isolation and security
isolation.desc: Three network modes: full isolation, API-only whitelist, or open access. Every request is visible in the monitor.
isolation.feat1: AI cannot reach the network without permission

memory.title: Dual-layer memory
memory.desc: Short-term (STM) and long-term (LTM) memory with semantic search and cross-chat/model transfer.

injection.title: Global injections
injection.desc: Unique feature: text automatically added to EVERY message — prefix, suffix, hidden context.

agents.title: AI agent teams
agents.desc: 10 orchestration strategies, 12 roles, 14 tools — full Agent Studio for multi-model teams.
agents.feat1: Sequential, parallel, hierarchical, MapReduce, and more
agents.feat2: Permissions: internet, files, delegation, veto, memory

permissions.title: Permissions & limits
permissions.desc: Per-chat and per-agent permissions: internet, devices, files, tools. RAM, CPU, and token limits.
permissions.feat1: Chat rights: STM, LTM, CHAT_ONLY / MODEL_SHARED / GLOBAL

innovation.title: Innovative technologies
innovation.desc: Silenium includes cutting-edge algorithms: neuroplastic memory, quantum context layers, holographic compression.

ready.title: All set!
ready.desc: Silenium is ready. All parameters can be changed in Settings — 150+ options available.
ready.feat2: Help with description of every feature

chat.generating: Model is generating (first run may take minutes)...
chat.internetSearchHint: With 🌐 on, Silenium fetches online data (weather via wttr.in, other queries via DuckDuckGo) and adds it to the prompt.
chat.teamParamsHint: Temperature, token limit, and RAM are set per agent in Agents.
chat.maxTokensReplyHint: Limit per single model reply (not context window). If the model loops, try 256–512.
chat.modeTeamDisabled: Create a team in Agents first
chat.singleModelHint: The model below generates replies in this chat.
chat.teamModelHint: Each agent uses its own model — chat-wide model selection is hidden.
chat.starterDownloadHint: Built-in lightweight model (~200 MB) for a quick start without Hugging Face.
chat.starterLimitHint: Starter (~360M) — for complex or slang questions, use a 1B+ Q4 GGUF model from Models.
chat.workspaceHint: Agents with file_read/file_write work only inside this folder and subfolders.
chat.systemPromptPh: Local instructions for this chat only...
chat.emptyDesc: Create a new chat to start a dialogue with a local model or agent team.
chat.sidebarEmpty: No chats yet. Click «New chat» to get started.
chat.welcomeStep1: Open ⚙ Chat properties — pick a model or agent team
chat.welcomeStep2: Download Silenium Starter from Models if you need a quick start
chat.welcomeStep3: Type a message — replies are generated entirely on your machine
chat.errorGeneric: Could not get a reply. Try again or check the model in settings. ({{err}})
chat.welcomeHint: ⚙ Chat properties — Single model vs Agent team, Starter download, and permissions. Camera and mic are at the bottom.
chat.liveHint: Live preview — send video frame and voice to chat
chat.enableLive: Enable camera and microphone in chat permissions

permTip.internet: Allow web search and HTTP for this chat

system.computeDeviceDesc: CPU — processor only; GPU — video card; Auto — based on VRAM and layers
system.gpuLayersDesc: 0 = auto from VRAM in GPU/Auto mode; ignored in CPU mode

innovation.title: Silenium innovative technologies
innovation.desc: Cutting-edge algorithms for context, memory, and cognitive load management.
innovation.cognitiveLoadBalancerDesc: Automatically distributes load across models by query complexity
innovation.neuroplasticDesc: Memory adapts to user's communication style
innovation.synapticRoutingDesc: Optimal processing path through neural network graph
innovation.contextDnaDesc: Unique genetic code of each chat's context
innovation.holographicDesc: Compress context into holographic multi-dimensional representation
innovation.thoughtStreamDesc: Streamed output plus a gentle think-first hint — no internal tags in the reply
innovation.thoughtMaxTokensDesc: Max tokens for the model reasoning (think) block
innovation.neuralMeshDesc: Sync between devices on local network
innovation.metaCognitionDesc: Model analyzes quality of its own responses

injection.title: Global per-message injections
injection.desc: Text automatically added to every message.
injection.hiddenContextDesc: Invisible to user, visible to model

inference.ggufRuntimeDesc: Silenium Core — built-in (recommended, no extra DLLs). Synaptic Auto — innovation routing. llama-cli — external process.
inference.contextDesc: How many tokens of history and prompt fit in model memory
inference.temperatureDesc: Higher = more creative; lower = more precise and predictable
inference.streamingDesc: Show the reply as it is generated, not all at once at the end
inference.runtimeDesc: Silenium Core is bundled in the app. llama-cli is only needed for Synaptic (latency) or llama-cli mode.
inference.runtimeBrowser: Available only in the Tauri desktop app.
inference.coreNoCliHint: Silenium Core mode does not require a separate llama-cli — native libs are bundled.

models.formats: GGUF, ONNX, Safetensors, PT, BIN, GGML

memory.title: Memory
memory.pageIntro: Manage short-term (STM) and long-term (LTM) memory: transfer between chats, consolidation, and access levels.

network.title: Network monitor
network.pageIntro: All outgoing HTTP requests appear here. In isolation mode, only whitelisted hosts are allowed.
network.noLogs: When the app uses the network, entries will show up here.
network.ddgDesc: DuckDuckGo Instant Answer API search with real-time activity panel
network.ddgTip: Requires internet permission in network settings

models.pageIntro: Add models from disk, download from Hugging Face, or use built-in Silenium Starter for a quick start.
models.emptyDesc: Drop a .gguf file in the models folder, download from Hugging Face, or install Silenium Starter on the Local tab.
models.noneLocal: Put GGUF/ONNX files in the models folder or click Browse
models.selectTip: Choose a loaded local model or built-in default
models.localHint: Copy the main model .gguf here (not mmproj). *mmproj* files are vision projectors only — they cannot chat.
models.hfDownloadNote: Real download: file is saved to models folder and verified by size.
models.hfEmpty: No results. Try query «gguf» or a model name.
models.hfTauriOnly: Hugging Face search is available in the built Silenium app.

help.title: Help
help.intro: Silenium — local AI runtime: 79+ settings in 9 categories, Agent Studio, AI memory, network isolation.

sections.system: RAM, CPU, GPU, priorities, and turbo mode.
sections.memory: STM (5–200 messages), LTM with importance, CHAT_ONLY / MODEL_SHARED / GLOBAL.
sections.injection: System prompt prepended to each message + global injections.
sections.agents: Agents section: 10 strategies, 12 roles, 14 tools, permissions and resources.
sections.devices: Camera, microphone, screen, files — multi-mode input.
sections.models: Models tab: folder ~/.local/share/silenium/models (Linux). Drop .gguf files there or download from Hugging Face. Each chat and agent member has its own model selector.

features.permissions: Permissions: internet, camera, mic, files, veto

agents.title: AI agent teams
agents.subtitle: Multi-model teamwork: roles, tools, orchestration, permissions
agents.emptyDesc: Create a team — several models with roles, tools, and shared orchestration. Then pick it in chat properties.
agents.monitorDesc: Visual orchestration: rounds, models, phases. Stop the team or individual agents.
agents.noEvents: Run a team task — events will appear here
agents.roleAutoPrompt: System role is filled from the selected role (edit text to disable auto-fill)
agents.pasteGroupError: Clipboard does not contain valid agent group JSON
agents.duplicateGroupTip: Create a copy of the group with new IDs

guide.title: Agent systems guide
guide.lead: A team is several models with roles, tools, and shared orchestration. In chat, pick a team under ⚙ Chat properties below the dialog.
guide.chatBody: Set «Agent team» in chat properties — each message runs the team and replies appear per agent. For tests without chat, use «Task for the team» in the editor.
guide.permsBody: Internet, screen, and files are per-agent. Browser and AI mouse need Screen + Browser automation / Desktop control in Settings → Permissions.
guide.rolesHint: Changing role auto-fills the system prompt until you edit it manually.
guide.toolsHint: file_read/file_write only work inside the chat workspace folder. Each agent can use a different GGUF model.
guide.workspaceBody: Set a folder in chat properties (⚙) — agents with Files permission read/write only inside it and subfolders.
guide.multiModelBody: Each agent uses its own model (text, code, vision). Summarizer or leader synthesizes one final answer.

tip.orchestration: 10 modes: sequential, round_robin, parallel, hierarchical, voting, debate, chain_of_thought, smart_router, map_reduce, expert_panel
tip.conflict: 6 modes: leader, voting, consensus, merge, retry, escalate

agentBrowser.title: Agent browser & AI mouse
agentBrowser.disabledHint: Enable «Browser automation» and «Desktop control» in Settings → Permissions → Devices.
agentBrowser.dualMouseHint: Your system mouse is untouched — the purple cursor belongs to the agent.
agentBrowser.liveHint: Live WebView: real page in a separate window; clicks via elementFromPoint / querySelector.
agentBrowser.previewHint: Preview: fetched HTML in iframe; link clicks use postMessage bridge.
